title: The Praise of the Cross
subtitle: An epilogue to the Passion: how His suffering makes Him lovable to us

stillness: Be still, my soul, and consider the time.
Attend and understand the most blessed time of the Passion.
Let the noise fall away; only one thing now is needed.
gospel: The just and innocent One is handed over and bound,
like a lamb led to the sacrifice,
like a thief led to the gallows.
Yet He seeks no revenge, He shows no impatience.
He could have asked the Father for twelve legions of angels,
yet He is bound like a thief, accused like a thief,
condemned like a thief, cast out like a thief,
punished among thieves, as though the prince of thieves.
He was wounded for our sins,
and at the ninth hour, with a great cry and with tears, He breathed His last.
behold: Attend and understand, O my soul, the time of His Passion.
Behold Him who suffered in every age, in every limb, in every hour.
In infancy: the narrow womb, the poverty and harshness of the manger, the flight into Egypt.
In youth: the most bitter and most shameful Passion.
Behold His whole body given over to pain:
in the eyes, the pouring out of tears;
in the ears, insults and blasphemies;
on the cheeks, the sting of blows;
in the nostrils, the stench of spittle;
in the mouth, the bitterness of vinegar and gall;
in the hands and feet, the nails and the wounds;
in the whole body, the scourging.
Behold the places of His pain: the manger, the desert, the temple, the garden, the hall, the praetorium, the place of crucifixion.
Behold the perversity, the boundless wickedness: the Judge is judged, the King is mocked, the High Priest is tortured.
consider: Consider now what the Lord suffered for the salvation of man.
These things He suffered for us make Him exceedingly lovable, and bind us more strongly to love Him.
His pain did not begin only at His seizing and betrayal;
it lasted from the instant of His conception until His death.
For He knew, with unfailing certainty, the most bitter death He had chosen to bear,
and so in every hour He naturally grieved over it.
Remember now, O man: though you were made out of nothing, you were not redeemed out of nothing.
In six days He made all things, and you among them;
but for thirty whole years He labored your salvation in the midst of the earth.
O how much He labored, enduring!
Bound, that He might loose the chains of sinners;
pierced with thorns, nails, and lance, that in us there might be upright purpose and clear love;
scourged, that He might drive from me the scourges of His wrath.
Weep then and grieve, O my soul, and let your eyes run down with tears,
and let them not be silent over your brother, exceedingly comely, lovely beyond the love of women.
His wounds are the effective medicine of our wounds.
prayer: who for the redemption of the world willed to bear patiently miseries and anguish,
reproaches and insults, slanders and injuries, pains and afflictions, the Passion and death:
through all these things which you endured for our sins,
free me from all sins and vices,
from all the perils of this world, from infernal punishments,
from sudden and from eternal death.
And grant to me, I beseech you,
not to flee or to forget all that you endured for me,
but to hold it always before my eyes, and to embrace it ardently;
so that, sharing in your labor and your sorrow,
you may will me also to be a partaker of your rest and your consolation.

latinJewel.latin: Plus itaque mihi contulit Deus redimendo, quam creando: creando quippe me mihi dedit, redimendo autem et seipsum dedit, et me mihi reddidit.
latinJewel.english: God gave me more in redeeming than in creating: for in creating He gave me to myself, but in redeeming He gave both Himself, and gave me back to myself.
